treatment_information.specific_treatment: total thyroidectomy and pre and para-tracheal dissection
treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Preservation of recurrent laryngeal nerves and parathyroids
treatment_information.treatment_response: Microscopic study showed tumor involvement in both lobes, medullary carcinoma type, without tumor necrosis, with macroscopic multicentric foci, with focal capsular invasion and no evidence of extrathyroid extension. Immunohistochemistry showed positivity for calcitonin, synaptophysin, CEA, and TTF-1 markers.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient referred to Genetics consultation due to family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC) and presence of cervical tumor.
Physical examination revealed hard nodules in both thyroid lobes.
Ultrasound examination of the thyroid showed solid, hypoechoic nodules with microcalcifications and vascularization, highly suspicious for malignancy.
Plasma calcitonin levels were elevated (1,605 pg/mL), establishing the diagnostic possibility of MTC.
Genetic testing for RET gene mutations was performed, identifying the pathogenic variant c.1888T>A (p.Cys630Ser) in exon 11.
Patient underwent total thyroidectomy and pre and para-tracheal dissection.
Post-surgery, patient was prescribed lifelong levothyroxine, calcium citrate, and vitamin D3.
Periodic analysis of calcitonin, parathyroid hormone, TSH, T4, and ionized calcium were performed, all within reference values.

patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Periodic analysis of calcitonin, parathyroid hormone, TSH, T4, and ionized calcium. Genetic counseling and testing recommended for at-risk family members.
